howItWorks.intro: This claude code cost estimator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Monthly Cost = Monthly Prompts × (Input Tokens × Input Rate + Output Tokens × Output Rate)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does Claude Code cost per month?
faq.answer: It depends on usage intensity. A typical developer using Sonnet with 10 prompts/hour for 6 hours/day might spend $50-150/month. Heavy Opus usage can be $500+/month.

faq.question: How do I reduce Claude Code costs?
faq.answer: Use Sonnet as your default model and switch to Opus only for complex tasks. Write clear, specific prompts to reduce back-and-forth. Use /compact to reduce context window size.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What coding hours/day should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate coding hours/day value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Claude Code Cost Estimator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Claude Code Cost Estimator?
sections.body: The Claude Code Cost Estimator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your coding hours/day, working days/month, prompts per hour, you get instant results including monthly prompts, monthly tokens, estimated monthly cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting monthly prompts right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your coding hours/day and need to find the right monthly prompts. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Coding Hours/Day
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your coding hours/day — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: working days/month, prompts per hour, avg input tokens, avg output tokens, primary model. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your monthly prompts, monthly tokens, estimated monthly cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your coding hours/day before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
